hook.text: Predicting income from age: for 20-year-olds, predictions are close. For 50-year-olds, they're wildly off.

buildup.text: When prediction error grows or shrinks with X, the variance isn't constant. That's heteroscedasticity.

discovery.text: Residual plots reveal it: a funnel or fan shape means the spread of errors changes across the range.

twist.text: The model's predictions are still unbiased, but confidence intervals and p-values become unreliable.

climax.text: Weighted regression or robust standard errors fix the problem without changing the model itself.

punchline.text: Same model, different reliability at different points.

quiz.question: What is heteroscedasticity?
options: When the spread of prediction errors changes across the range of the predictor
When all errors are the same size
When the regression line is perfectly horizontal
